text:
Admission Date:  2006-01-08       Discharge Date:  2010-05-07


Service:  NEUROSURGERY

PRIMARY DIAGNOSIS:  Right cerebellar hemorrhage.

SECONDARY DIAGNOSIS:
1.  Coronary artery disease.
2.  Hypertension.
3.  Atrial fibrillation.
4.  Congestive heart failure.
5.  Osteoarthritis.
6.  Diverticulitis.
7.  Gout.

HISTORY OF THE PRESENT ILLNESS:  The patient is an
89-year-old lady who presented on 2006-01-08 with nausea,
vomiting, dizziness, worsening with movement, slurred speech,
and headache which was worsening with movement.  She has a
history of atrial fibrillation and has been on Coumadin
treatment.  She denied any history of fall.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION ON ADMISSION:  Vital signs:  She was
afebrile.  The vital signs were stable, although the blood
pressure was elevated at 200/90.  General:  She was slightly
sleepy but oriented times three.  Neurologic:  On
examination, cranial nerves II through XII were intact.  She
was strong bilaterally throughout the upper and lower
extremities and sensory functions were all normal.  Her
reflexes were normal and plantar reflexes were downgoing.  In
summary, she was neurologically intact.

LABORATORY/RADIOLOGIC DATA:  Her laboratories were
unremarkable except for an elevated INR of 2.3.

A head CT showed a right cerebellar bleed with no mass affect
and no shift.

HOSPITAL COURSE:  She was admitted to the ICU for monitoring
and given 6 units of fresh frozen plasma to correct her INR.
Her stay in the ICU was uneventful.  She was transferred to
the floor on 1997-07-02.  Her INR was corrected at 1.3 to
1.4.

An ECG showed atrial fibrillation which was rate controlled.
Chest x-ray displayed mild cardiomegaly, a left pleural
effusion, and signs of CHF.

She is currently on ondansetron 2-4 mg IV q. six hours,
calcium gluconate 2 grams per 100 mils, D5W IV, magnesium
sulfate 2 grams per 100 mils of D5W, digoxin 0.125 mg p.o.
O.D., metoprolol 15 mg p.o. t.i.d., quinapril 20 mg p.o.
O.D., Allopurinol 100 mg p.o. O.D., colchicine 0.6 mg p.o. O.D.,
hydralazine 25 mg p.o. q. 6 hours to keep blood pressure below
150, Isosorbide dinitrate 10 mg p.o. b.i.d., erythromycin 0.5%
ophthalmologic ointment, miconazole powder 2%.

The patient is currently stable and awaiting transfer to the
Nursing and Rehabilitation tomorrow.
She has no limitations to her activity nor her diet.




                         DR.Giles,Travis 14-133

Dictated By:[**Dictator Info **]
